file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/665.lean` — Erdős Problem 665

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–33).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 665`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/665`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (pairwise balanced designs).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[ErLa82]`, `[ShSi85]`, `[Er97f]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos665 ... end Erdos665`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos665`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 35).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsPairwiseBalancedDesign` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 39–41).
- [x] Definition is `Prop`-valued, no `noncomputable` needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Type uses `UpperCamelCase` (`IsPairwiseBalancedDesign`).
- [x] No local notation used (none needed).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity check on `IsPairwiseBalancedDesign`. *Optional — consider adding a test to verify the definition behaves as expected on small examples.*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_665` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (line 56).
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 46–55).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[ErLa82]`, `[Er97f]`, `[ShSi85]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Is there a constant C > 0 such that …?" — a yes/no question. **Case 1** applies.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (existence of constant C such that large enough n admits a PBD with all blocks of size > √n − C), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ C`, `∀ n`) are **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single entry).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses the multi-line `by` / `sorry` format (`:= by` on line 62, `sorry` indented on line 63) — acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations (only in `answer(sorry)` placeholder and proof body).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `≤`, `≠`, `↔`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `≥`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All substantive checklist items pass. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, definition, theorem statement, `answer()` usage (Case 1 — yes/no question), category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality are all conformant.

Minor optional suggestion: consider adding a `@[category test]` sanity check for `IsPairwiseBalancedDesign` on a small example (e.g., the trivial PBD on `Fin 4`).

content.text: The review result is **PASS**. No changes needed.
